title: WHERE condition is incorrectly dropped when SELECT alias matches filtered column name
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
ClickHouse incorrectly optimizes away a WHERE condition when the filtered column name matches an aliased constant in the SELECT clause. This violates SQL standard behavior where column references in WHERE should be independent of SELECT aliases.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
CREATE TABLE test (platform UInt8, uid UInt64) ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO test VALUES (9, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3);
-- Expected: Returns 2 (should exclude platform=9)
-- Actual: Returns 3 (WHERE condition is ignored)
SELECT 0 as platform, COUNT(DISTINCT uid) FROM test WHERE platform != 9;
-- Works correctly when alias differs:
SELECT 0 as plat, COUNT(DISTINCT uid) FROM test WHERE platform != 9; -- Correctly returns 2
### Expected behavior
Both queries should return 2 rows (excluding platform=9), regardless of the SELECT alias naming.
The first query ignores the WHERE platform != 9 condition when the alias matches the filtered column name.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
ClickHouse version: 24.3.2
Server OS: docker  (bitnami/clickhouse)
Client interface: dbeaver
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test1 (platform SMALLINT, uid BIGINT);
SELECT 0 as platform_alias, COUNT(DISTINCT uid) FROM test1 WHERE platform != 9;
CREATE TABLE test2 (id INTEGER, value TEXT);
INSERT INTO test2 (id, value) VALUES (1, 'A'), (2, NULL), (3, 'B');
key_bug_features: WHERE condition dropped with matching SELECT alias
